title: Krea2: replace the Turbo transformers with RAW + accelerator LoRA — collapse 4 model cards to 2
description:
GOAL: drop the two Turbo transformers and run RAW + the Krea2 turbo-distill accelerator LoRA instead, collapsing the Krea2 model library from 4 cards to 2 (raw-sfw + raw-nsfw). Every user then gets BOTH a fast tier and a quality tier from whichever card they installed, instead of paying for a second ~12GB transformer to get the other speed mode.

WHY (the real driver — corrected 2026-07-20, the original card framing was wrong): users report Turbo LOCKS UP. Vague prompt + new seed => near-identical result. Turbo trades creative diversity for landing a good image in few steps. Users are already reaching for RAW + a speed LoRA instead of the Turbo card. The ~24.5GB disk saving is the bonus, not the point.

STATUS: A/B RUN AND PASSED (2026-07-20). RAW SFW + accelerator LoRA @ strength 1.0 reproduces Turbo seed-for-seed at LOWER wall-clock, on BOTH an anime prompt and a photoreal prompt. Full evidence, grids and timings: research/01-turbo-lora-parity.md. Card now moves from 'does this work' to 'design + ship the single-card shape'.

THE LORA: krea2_turbo_lora_rank_64_bf16.safetensors (Comfy-Org/Krea-2) — 469MB, rank 64, BF16. Its safetensors metadata (on the TheDivergentAI twin) states base_model krea/Krea-2-Raw, target_model krea/Krea-2-Turbo, extraction_method svd_lowrank_weight_delta. It is Turbo extracted as a delta FROM Raw, so RAW + LoRA@1.0 reconstructs Turbo BY CONSTRUCTION — that is why parity was expected and found. TheDivergentAI/krea2-turbo-distill-lora r64 is the same extraction in lora_A/lora_B key convention; both tested, output near-identical (24s vs 20s). r128/r256 exist (lower reconstruction error) but were not needed for parity.

SEPARATE FINDING — THE BYPASS LORA (deserves its own card): krea2filterbypass3.safetensors (160 bytes, ONE tensor: a 12-float32 delta on diffusion_model.txtfusion.projector.diff) suppresses the watermarks that stock Krea2 RAW bakes into photoreal output. Watermarks come from the BASE Krea2 RAW weights, NOT from the Lustify NSFW finetune — this was verified on stock SFW RAW. Turbo does not watermark. It is a text-fusion-projector override named 'filterbypass'; if it ships on-by-default for all RAW users that is a deliberate decision to document as what it is, not as a dewatermark utility. Evidence is n=1 per cell — seed-repeat before shipping.

STILL TO DECIDE BEFORE SHIPPING:
- Negative-prompt gating per tier. Today Input_Negative (node 283) is gated STRUCTURALLY: the cfg-1 tiers compute and discard it. Collapse to ONE card and that free gating disappears. Either show the negative UI only on the guided tier, or accept a dead control on the fast tiers — but do not let it become an invisible no-op the user can type into.
- Identity-edit path (MPI-282): RAW exists because it has a working cfg to drive the edit LoRA. Verify the accelerator LoRA does not break edit, or scope edit to the guided tier explicitly.
- Shared-dep guard: removing two transformers touches install/uninstall accounting. Read the MPI-276 invariants first (memory project_shared_dep_guard_exclusive_evidence).
- Style LoRA composition: style LoRAs + the edit LoRA are known NOT to compose (MPI-282). Accelerator is a third stack member — verify against both.
- int8 vs fp8 quant: all RAW arms ran krea2_raw_int8_convrot, Turbo ran fp8_scaled. docs/models/krea2/int8-quant.md checkbox 4 (quality A/B) is still unrun. Parity held anyway, but the confound is unretired.

ACCEPTANCE:
- DONE: A/B run exists — RAW+LoRA vs Turbo, same prompt/seed/resolution, images and wall-clock in research/01-turbo-lora-parity.md
- DONE: written verdict on whether quality holds — PASS at strength 1.0, both prompt modes
- If it proceeds: negative-prompt behaviour per tier is an explicit, documented decision, not an accident of cfg
- If it proceeds: identity-edit still works (or edit is deliberately scoped to the guided tier)
- If it proceeds: models.js/modelDeps.js collapse to 2 Krea2 cards with a runtime tier radio; Turbo weights removed from R2

READ FIRST: research/01-turbo-lora-parity.md (this card's own evidence), docs/models/krea2/samplers.md, docs/models/krea2/injection.md, docs/models/krea2/int8-quant.md, js/data/modelConstants/modelDeps.js. Related: MPI-282 (RAW exists for edit cfg), MPI-300 (the tier-radio shape to copy), MPI-276 (shared-dep guard invariants).
